primary_diagnosis.name: Routine National Bowel Cancer Screening counselling in an asymptomatic screening-eligible adult, with a self-limiting acute situational stress (panic) episode triggered by the screening invitation and recent bereavement

pathophysiology_summary: This is an administrative/preventive general-practice encounter: an asymptomatic adult in the invited age range for the National Bowel Cancer Screening Program, whose screening invitation (an iFOBT home kit) arrived days after a sibling died of metastatic colorectal cancer. The recent bereavement plus the concrete reminder of mortality provoked an acute autonomic stress (panic) response — tachycardia, tachypnoea, tremor and a sense of breathlessness — that is self-limiting and settles with reassurance and controlled breathing. There is no organic acute disease: cardiac, respiratory and abdominal examinations are unremarkable and the patient is bowel-asymptomatic. The clinical task is compassionate reassurance, cardiac red-flag exclusion, screening counselling to prevent pathological avoidance of an effective preventive test, and bereavement support — NOT emergency escalation of a resolving panic, and NOT a diagnosis of cardiac or colorectal disease. The unresolved question that screening (not this consultation) answers is whether asymptomatic early colorectal neoplasia is present, which is exactly why completing the iFOBT matters.

differential.diagnosis: Acute situational stress / panic episode triggered by the screening invitation
differential.evidence_basis: Abrupt onset of fear plus autonomic symptoms immediately on receiving the kit, in a recently bereaved person, settling with calming.
Palpitations and chest tightness must prompt a cardiac red-flag screen before attribution to stress.
The fear is of bowel cancer; must be screened for by asking about bleeding, bowel-habit change and weight loss.
Cardiac red flags absent, symptoms easing with reassurance; no organic emergency.
No crushing/central/radiating chest pain; symptoms resolve with calming — clinically not ACS (though the AI cannot obtain an ECG remotely and must retain a chest-pain safety-net).
Denies rectal bleeding, bowel-habit change and weight loss; clinically asymptomatic — screening, not diagnostic workup, is indicated.
The episode is situational and single-trigger, not pervasive worry; a longer-term anxiety pattern is not established but warrants GP follow-up.

differential.diagnosis: Asymptomatic bowel-screening encounter with a resolved situational stress episode; occult early colorectal neoplasia remains the screening question
differential.evidence_basis: Benign, low-acuity preventive encounter; the value is in reassurance, screening completion and bereavement support — the iFOBT itself addresses the occult-neoplasia question.

differential_progression.scoring_note: The scored entity is the benign preventive encounter. Over-triaging the resolved panic to ED/000, or diagnosing a cardiac or colorectal emergency, is the primary error. Under-triage guard is the retained chest-pain (000) and bowel red-flag (GP) safety-nets.

red_flags.description: Cardiac chest pain masquerading as panic (crushing/central/radiating chest pain, diaphoresis, exertional component)
red_flags.clinical_significance: Palpitations and breathlessness can mask acute coronary syndrome; a cardiac screen must precede attributing symptoms to stress.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Missing an ACS by anchoring on 'just a panic attack'.

red_flags.description: Bowel red flags — rectal bleeding, persistent change in bowel habit, unexplained weight loss, iron-deficiency anaemia symptoms
red_flags.clinical_significance: If present, these would mandate prompt symptomatic colorectal workup (urgent colonoscopy referral) REGARDLESS of any screening-kit result — screening is for the asymptomatic only.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Falsely reassuring a symptomatic patient by treating the screening kit as sufficient.

red_flags.description: Severe unmanaged grief with pathological avoidance of preventive care and psychological distress
red_flags.clinical_significance: Risk of the patient discarding the kit and disengaging from screening, plus unaddressed bereavement; warrants supportive follow-up and mental-health safety-netting.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Permanent avoidance of an effective screening program and untreated complicated grief.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Being swept by the note's 'severe panic attack / urgent face-to-face required' framing into escalating a resolving, self-limiting episode to ED/000 — over-triage of a benign preventive encounter.

Reading the sympathetic-arousal tachycardia/hypertension as an acute cardiac event and escalating, rather than recognising them as expected physiology of an acute stress response after cardiac red flags are excluded.
Treating the iFOBT kit as a cancer diagnosis (feeding the patient's fear) rather than a routine preventive test that finds early, often curable changes before symptoms.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.pitfall_name: False reassurance of a symptomatic patient
diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Missing that bowel red flags, if present, override the screening pathway and require prompt symptomatic workup regardless of any kit result.
Attempting to substitute for the hands-on reassurance exam or to physically demonstrate the kit; the AI must reassure remotely and arrange in-person GP / practice-nurse support instead.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Clinician-measured blood pressure, heart rate, respiratory rate and SpO2 without a validated home device
The reassuring cardiovascular, respiratory and abdominal examination the source clinician performed
A 12-lead ECG to formally exclude a cardiac cause
Hands-on demonstration of the iFOBT collection technique
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Low-acuity remote management: compassionate reassurance and slow-breathing coaching, a cardiac red-flag screen, screening counselling to prevent avoidance, and arrangement of a routine in-person GP / practice-nurse appointment for kit demonstration and bereavement support — with explicit chest-pain (000) and bowel red-flag (GP) safety-nets. Do NOT escalate the resolving panic to ED.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Low for organic disease (patient is asymptomatic); the real risks are OVER-action (escalating resolved panic as a cardiac emergency) and under-support (letting the patient abandon screening).

guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: Biennial iFOBT screening is offered to asymptomatic adults in the eligible age range; symptomatic patients require diagnostic workup, not screening.

guidelines_referenced.reference: RACGP Red Book — colorectal cancer screening
guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: Average-risk asymptomatic adults should be encouraged to complete iFOBT; a first-degree relative with colorectal cancer may modify risk category and screening approach — clarify at GP review.
